pattern: Whatever needs to happen to the leftmost remaining nonzero element must happen through an operation window starting exactly there, so sweeping left to right and applying that forced window (tracked lazily) either works all the way through or proves it is impossible.
hints: The leftmost element that is still above zero can only ever be brought down by an operation whose window starts exactly at its position -- any window starting earlier would have already been forced to start even earlier.

Track the effect of windows you have already committed to with a running total (or a difference array) instead of mutating every element in the window each time, so you don't pay for the full window width at every step.
If the forced window at some position would run past the end of the array, or if some element ever goes negative, it is impossible; otherwise reaching the end with everything at zero means it worked.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, achievable with three operations
single element window equal to array length
window equal to the whole array with equal values
whole-array window forces every element to move together, but they are not all equal
a late nonzero element has no room left for a full window
already all zero, no operations needed
decreasing values with a window smaller than the array

stress.description: random arrays and window sizes, roughly half satisfiable and half not
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 1000000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums, k]
